error.pairing_failed: Pairing failed; please check the Bosch Smart Home Controller is in pairing mode (LED flashing) as well as your password is correct.

confirm_discovery.description:
Smart Home Controller I: Please press the front-side button until LED starts flashing.
Smart Home Controller II: Press the function button shortly. Cloud and network lights start blinking orange.
Device is now ready to be paired.

Ready to continue to set up {model} @ {host} with Home Assistant?

user.description: Set up your Bosch Smart Home Controller to allow monitoring and control with Home Assistant.
user.title: SHC authentication parameters

init.title: Bosch SHC — Settings
init.description: Configure which entities and automations the Bosch Smart Home Controller integration creates and manages.{camera_tool}

features.name: Features
features.description: Toggle the entity types the integration creates.

data.all_light_switches_as_light: Expose ALL Light/Shutter Control II as lights
data.light_switches_as_light: Expose Light/Shutter Control II as lights
data.suppress_hue_lights: Suppress Hue lights from Bosch SHC
data.suppress_ledvance_lights: Suppress LEDVANCE lights from Bosch SHC

data_description.scenarios_as_buttons: When ON: each Bosch SHC scenario also appears as a button entity in Home Assistant (in addition to the `trigger_scenario` service). Useful for dashboard tiles and automations that trigger scenarios directly. Default: off.
data_description.diagnostic_entities: When ON (default): diagnostic sensor entities are created for each supported device — valve tappet position (thermostats) and communication quality (smart plugs). Turn OFF to reduce entity count if you do not need these sensors.
data_description.enable_rawscan_service: When ON: registers the `bosch_shc.trigger_rawscan` service for raw API debugging. Turn OFF to hide it. Default: on.
data_description.all_light_switches_as_light: Turn ON to present every Light Control II / in-wall light-switch device as a `light` entity at once — overrides the per-device selection below. Off (default) keeps the per-device choice. Same caveat: this swaps the entity domain (`switch.*` ↔ `light.*`), so update automations/dashboards that reference the old entities.
data_description.light_switches_as_light: Select the Light Control II / in-wall light-switch devices that should appear as `light` entities instead of `switch` entities. Each selected device's switch is replaced by an on/off light. Leave empty (default) to keep them as switches. Changing this swaps the entity domain (`switch.*` ↔ `light.*`), so update any automations or dashboards that reference the old entity.
data_description.suppress_hue_lights: When ON: Hue lights paired to the SHC are no longer exposed as entities by this integration. Use this to avoid duplicates when the Hue integration (https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/hue/) is also active. Default: off (Hue lights are shown).
data_description.suppress_power_sensors: When ON: power and energy sensors (watt / kWh) are not created for smart plugs, BSM switches, micromodule controls, and the EMMA energy manager. Default: off (sensors are shown).
data_description.scenarios_filter: Leave empty to create buttons for ALL scenarios (default). Select one or more to limit buttons to only those scenarios. Applies only when 'Expose scenarios as button entities' is on.
data_description.suppress_ledvance_lights: When ON: LEDVANCE lights paired to the SHC are no longer exposed as entities by this integration. Use this to avoid duplicates when the LEDVANCE or Zigbee integration is also active. Default: off.
data_description.suppress_motion_indicator_light: When ON: the indicator-LED brightness entity for Motion Detector II devices is not created. Default: off (entity is shown).
data_description.suppress_camera_switches: When ON: privacy-mode, light, and notification switch entities for Bosch cameras (Eyes / 360 / Outdoor Gen2) are not created. Use this when the Bosch Camera Tool (https://github.com/mosandlt/Bosch-Smart-Home-Camera-Tool-HomeAssistant) is also active to avoid duplicate controls. Default: off.

presence.name: Presence-based child lock & silent mode
presence.description: Automatically lock all SHC thermostats/switches when someone is home, and optionally switch thermostats to silent mode during a time window when someone is home.

data_description.child_lock_enabled: Master switch. When ON, child lock on all SHC thermostats and switch devices follows the presence entities below — locked when someone is home, unlocked when everyone is away. When OFF, the integration never touches child lock.
data_description.child_lock_presence_entity: The people/devices that decide child lock. It turns ON as soon as ANY of them is home, and OFF only when ALL are away. Pick e.g. the Person entities of your household. 'Home' is detected automatically (home for person/device_tracker/zone/group, on for binary_sensor/input_boolean).
data_description.silent_mode_enabled: When ON, every silent-mode-capable SHC thermostat is switched to silent mode while someone (per the presence entities above) is home AND the current time is inside the window below; otherwise back to normal. Default: off.
data_description.silent_mode_start: Start of the daily silent-mode window (local time). Windows may cross midnight (e.g. 22:00 → 06:00).
data_description.silent_mode_end: End of the daily silent-mode window (local time).

advanced.name: Advanced
advanced.description: Low-level connection parameters. Changing these rarely helps — consult the docs before touching them.

data_description.ssl_verify_hostname: Expert: when ON, the SHC's TLS certificate hostname is verified. The Bosch SHC certificate's hostname normally does NOT match its IP, so enabling this usually breaks the connection — leave OFF unless you know your setup matches. Default: off.
data_description.long_poll_timeout: How long each long-poll request waits for events before reconnecting. Default 10. Increase to reduce reconnects, decrease if your network drops idle connections.
data_description.excluded_devices: Devices to hide — no entities are created for them. Leave empty to include all. Existing entities for newly-excluded devices remain until removed manually.
data_description.excluded_rooms: Hide every device in these Bosch rooms. Leave empty to include all.
data_description.ssl_skip_verify: Expert / local-only LANs: when ON, the SHC server certificate is no longer verified — useful if your offline controller's certificate has expired. The encrypted client-certificate login is unaffected. Only enable on a trusted local network. Default: off.

smokedetector_check.name: Smoke Detector Check
smokedetector_check.description: Calls the test routine of a smoke detector.

entity_id.name: Entity ID
entity_id.description: Name of the smoke detector entity.

trigger_scenario.name: Trigger Scenario
trigger_scenario.description: Triggers a scenario on Bosch SHC.

name.name: Scenario name
name.description: Name of the scenario to trigger

trigger_rawscan.name: Trigger Rawscan
trigger_rawscan.description: Triggers a rawscan on Bosch SHC to discover devices. Result is streamed to the info logger.

command.name: Rawscan command
command.description: Rawscan command to trigger. Possible commands: 'devices', 'device', 'services', 'device_services', 'device_service', 'rooms', 'scenarios', 'info', 'information', 'public_information', 'intrusion_detection'

device_id.name: Device ID
device_id.description: Device ID — required for commands 'device', 'device_services', or 'device_service'

smokedetector_alarmstate.name: Smoke Detector Alarm State
smokedetector_alarmstate.description: Send a custom command to a smoke detector.

entity_id.name: Entity ID
entity_id.description: Name of the smoke detector entity.

command.name: Custom command
command.description: Command string to send to the smoke detector.

camera_tool_available.title: More for your Bosch cameras
camera_tool_available.description: Bosch cameras were detected. This integration covers the basics; the dedicated **Bosch Smart Home Camera Tool** does much more for them (snapshots, motion / FCM push, lights, streams). Open "Learn more" for the project, then dismiss this notice.
